title: Statistics
summary: Chapter 14 covers statistical measures for grouped data. Students learn three methods to find the mean of grouped data: direct method, assumed mean method, and step deviation method. The chapter covers finding the mode using the modal class formula and finding the median using the cumulative frequency method and ogive. Students also learn to draw ogives and find median graphically.
learningPoints: Calculate mean of grouped data using Direct Method: x̄ = Σfᵢxᵢ / Σfᵢ
Calculate mean using Assumed Mean Method: x̄ = a + Σfᵢdᵢ/Σfᵢ
Calculate mean using Step Deviation Method: x̄ = a + (Σfᵢuᵢ/Σfᵢ) × h
Identify the modal class as the class with highest frequency
Apply mode formula: Mode = l + [(f₁-f₀)/(2f₁-f₀-f₂)] × h
Calculate cumulative frequencies to find median class
Apply median formula: Median = l + [(n/2-cf)/f] × h
Draw a less than ogive and more than ogive
Find median graphically using intersection of two ogives

formulas.label: Mean (Direct Method)
formulas.description: Mean of grouped data where xᵢ is midpoint and fᵢ is frequency of each class.

formulas.label: Mean (Assumed Mean)
formulas.expression: x̄ = a + Σfᵢdᵢ/Σfᵢ, where dᵢ = xᵢ - a
formulas.description: Mean using assumed mean 'a' to simplify calculations.

formulas.label: Mean (Step Deviation)
formulas.description: Most efficient method using class width h and assumed mean a.

formulas.label: Mode Formula
formulas.description: l=lower class limit of modal class, f₁=modal frequency, f₀=preceding, f₂=following, h=class width.

formulas.label: Median Formula
formulas.description: l=lower limit of median class, n=total frequency, cf=preceding cumulative frequency, f=median class frequency.

formulas.label: Empirical Relationship
formulas.description: Approximate relationship between the three measures of central tendency.

resources.label: Statistics — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on mean, median, mode for grouped data and ogives.

mcqs.question: The class with the highest frequency is called:
mcqs.explanation: Modal class is the class interval with the highest frequency.

mcqs.question: In step deviation method, uᵢ = (xᵢ - a)/h. If a=25, h=10, xᵢ=45, then uᵢ is:
Total students = 50. For median, n/2 is:
mcqs.explanation: n/2 = 50/2 = 25. We find the class where cumulative frequency first exceeds 25.

mcqs.question: Empirical relationship between mean, median and mode is:
In the mode formula l + [(f₁-f₀)/(2f₁-f₀-f₂)]×h, f₁ represents:
mcqs.explanation: f₁ is the frequency of the modal class (highest frequency class).

mcqs.question: Median class is found using:
options: B. Cumulative frequency table where cf crosses n/2
mcqs.explanation: Median class is the class whose cumulative frequency first equals or exceeds n/2.

mcqs.question: If mean = 10 and mode = 7, then median is approximately:
In less than ogive, the y-axis shows:
mcqs.explanation: In ogive (cumulative frequency curve), y-axis represents cumulative frequency.

mcqs.question: Assumed mean method is useful because:
options: C. Works only for small data
mcqs.explanation: Assumed mean method simplifies arithmetic by working with smaller deviations from the assumed mean.

mcqs.question: The median is graphically determined from:
options: C. Intersection point of two ogives
mcqs.explanation: The x-coordinate of intersection of less than and more than ogives gives the median.

mcqs.question: For classes 0-10, 10-20, 20-30. Frequencies 4, 6, 5. Mean by direct method:
Which method is most efficient when class widths are equal?
mcqs.explanation: Step deviation method is most efficient when class widths are equal as it minimizes calculations.

mcqs.question: In median formula, 'cf' stands for:
options: B. Cumulative frequency of the class
C. Cumulative frequency of class preceding median class
mcqs.explanation: cf = cumulative frequency of all classes preceding (before) the median class.

mcqs.question: A less than ogive and more than ogive always intersect at the point corresponding to:
mcqs.explanation: The intersection of the two ogives gives the median of the distribution.

exercises.question: Find mean of: Class 0-10(f=5), 10-20(f=8), 20-30(f=15), 30-40(f=12), 40-50(f=10) using direct method.
Mean and mode of data are 59 and 52 respectively. Find median using empirical formula.
The following table gives literacy rates. Find mean using step deviation method: 45-55(f=3), 55-65(f=10), 65-75(f=11), 75-85(f=8), 85-95(f=3).
Draw less than ogive for: 0-10(f=5), 10-20(f=8), 20-30(f=12), 30-40(f=10), 40-50(f=5) and find median.
exercises.answer: Cumulative frequencies: less than 10→5, 20→13, 30→25, 40→35, 50→40. Plot points (10,5),(20,13),(30,25),(40,35),(50,40). n/2=20. Draw horizontal line at y=20. Meets curve at approximately x=27.5. Median ≈ 27.5.
